skill.name: api-design-principles
skill.description: Design REST and GraphQL APIs with practical guidance for resources, schemas, pagination, errors, versioning, and developer-facing documentation.
skill.summary: Plan, review, and document REST and GraphQL API contracts with structured design guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found no malicious intent, no prompt injection, and no real shell execution despite many static alerts. Most static findings are false positives from Markdown examples, but the FastAPI template includes permissive host and CORS defaults that need a publication warning.

medium_findings.title: Permissive Network Defaults in FastAPI Template
medium_findings.description: The template labels itself production-ready while allowing all trusted hosts and all CORS origins. The comments warn users to configure these settings, so this is not malicious, but copying the template unchanged could expose an API to unwanted hosts or origins. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: Direct code evidence shows wildcard host and origin settings in an executable FastAPI template; risk is moderated by nearby TODO comments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct code evidence shows wildcard host and origin settings in an executable FastAPI template. Risk is moderated by nearby TODO comments that instruct users to restrict these values.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alerts in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear on headings, REST and GraphQL prose, status-code examples, and API terminology. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithms being implemented or recommended. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed representative flagged lines and found documentation text rather than cryptographic code or guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed representative flagged lines and found documentation text rather than cryptographic code. No evidence found for weak cryptographic implementation or recommendations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings come from Markdown fences and inline code formatting used for REST, GraphQL, and Python examples. These files do not execute the fenced content, and I found no shell invocation path. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation delimiters or inline references, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown delimiters or inline file references. No evidence found for shell execution, subprocess calls, or Ruby backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Example URLs and Server Bind Address
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts are example pagination links, and the hardcoded IP is a local runnable server binding in a template. These are not data exfiltration, but the bind address should be reviewed before deployment. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The URL evidence is clearly inside REST documentation examples; the IP binding is executable but local to the sample app.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL evidence is clearly inside REST documentation examples. The IP binding is executable but local to the sample app and not paired with exfiltration logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Wildcard Host and CORS Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The template uses wildcard host and origin settings in a runnable FastAPI app. This is a risky default for production deployments and should be replaced with explicit hostnames and origins. Confidence: 0.80. Confidence reasoning: The pattern is directly present in executable Python code, although comments indicate it is intended for user customization.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in executable Python code. Nearby comments reduce concern because they tell users to configure safer production values.

content.user_title: Design Better REST and GraphQL APIs
content.value_statement: API contracts often become inconsistent, hard to version, and difficult for developers to use. This skill gives structured REST and GraphQL guidance for clearer resources, schemas, errors, pagination, and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Guides REST resource modeling, HTTP method use, status codes, pagination, filtering, and versioning decisions.
Explains GraphQL schema patterns for types, inputs, payloads, pagination, directives, errors, and DataLoader usage.
Provides an API design checklist for pre-implementation and specification reviews.
Includes a FastAPI REST template with models, endpoints, pagination, and structured errors.
Helps compare REST and GraphQL tradeoffs for product and engineering constraints.
limitations: It does not generate a complete production API without project-specific security and data-layer work.
The FastAPI template uses placeholder data and must be hardened before deployment.
It does not replace formal threat modeling, load testing, or compliance review.
It focuses on REST and GraphQL, not gRPC, AsyncAPI, or event contract design.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Public API
use_cases.description: Define resources, versioning, pagination, error formats, and documentation standards before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Review an API Specification
use_cases.description: Check an OpenAPI or GraphQL proposal for consistency, developer usability, security assumptions, and long-term maintainability.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team API Guidelines
use_cases.description: Create shared rules for REST endpoints, GraphQL schemas, errors, rate limits, and deprecation workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic REST Endpoints
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small REST API plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these REST endpoints for naming, HTTP method choice, status codes, pagination, and error handling. Explain the highest-impact fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Resource Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a new API surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a resource model for this product workflow. Include endpoint names, key request fields, response shapes in prose, pagination, filtering, and versioning choices.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a GraphQL Schema
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of schema structure and resolver risk.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this GraphQL schema for type design, nullability, mutation payloads, pagination, authorization boundaries, query complexity, and N+1 risks.

prompt_templates.title: Create API Standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced team-wide governance and migration planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create API design standards for my team. Cover REST and GraphQL conventions, breaking-change policy, error taxonomy, security requirements, examples, and review gates.

output_examples.input: A team asks whether its user and order endpoints follow REST conventions.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review that flags inconsistent resource names, missing pagination, unclear error formats, and versioning gaps, followed by revised endpoint recommendations.

output_examples.input: A product group needs a GraphQL schema review before implementation.
output_examples.output: A schema critique covering nullability, input and payload patterns, connection-style pagination, authorization placement, and DataLoader requirements.

output_examples.input: A platform team wants shared API guidelines.
output_examples.output: A concise standards document covering naming, methods, status codes, errors, authentication, rate limits, documentation, deprecation, and review checklists.

best_practices: Start with consumer workflows, then model resources, types, and operations around stable domain concepts.
Define errors, pagination, authentication, rate limits, and versioning before implementation starts.
Review examples from the client perspective to catch confusing names, missing fields, and breaking-change risks.
anti_patterns: Do not mirror database tables directly as public API resources without product-level design review.
Do not ship wildcard CORS, broad host access, or placeholder authentication in production APIs.
Do not remove or rename public fields without a documented deprecation and migration path.

faq.question: Does this skill write production API code?
faq.answer: It provides design guidance and templates. Production code still needs project-specific security, persistence, testing, and deployment work.

faq.question: Can it review both REST and GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes REST best practices, GraphQL schema patterns, and a shared API design checklist.

faq.question: Does it support OpenAPI documentation?
faq.answer: It discusses OpenAPI documentation practices and includes FastAPI examples that can generate OpenAPI docs.

faq.question: Is the included FastAPI template ready to deploy?
faq.answer: No. The template is a starting point and must be hardened, connected to real storage, and tested before deployment.

faq.question: Can it help with API versioning decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares URL, header, and query-parameter versioning and explains deprecation strategy.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
